theorem_target: One fine-token multinomial confidence event simultaneously certifies every same-table-selected stochastic release channel under a prespecified common-transform plus bounded differential-contamination shift model.
claim_boundary: The confirmation tests finite-sample coverage and usefulness under known synthetic populations in the stated shift class. It does not establish real-data shift membership, clinical safety, novelty, or superiority to code that was not run.

pilot_exclusion.legacy_limitation: The earliest design artifact predates channel-level receipts and is preserved only as aggregate design history; it is excluded from evidence and all confirmatory estimates.
pilot_exclusion.use: The disclosed pilot selected only bounded scenarios, thresholds, sample sizes, and the finite LTT grid. No pilot row enters a confirmatory estimate or interval.

population.strata: Two labels by two sources, with exactly n independent multinomial draws in every source-label stratum.

scenarios.name: hard_safety_boundary
scenarios.role: Tests whether an uncorrected plug-in continuum optimizer falsely accepts a near-boundary external contract while MOSAIC abstains.

scenarios.name: retention_and_stochastic_value
scenarios.role: Tests same-table sample efficiency and the strict value of stochastic rather than deterministic release channels.

methods.always_deploy_plugin: Exact empirical external-risk continuum optimum, deployed regardless of empirical utility.
methods.plugin_continuum: Exact empirical external-risk continuum optimum, deployed only when empirical privacy and utility pass, with no uncertainty correction.
methods.heldout_fixed_channel: Half-sample exact empirical channel selection followed by direct simultaneous one-sided Hoeffding tests for the fixed channel and decoder on the untouched half; there is no candidate-count penalty.
methods.finite_ltt: A fixed 5-by-5-by-5 stochastic-channel grid and four decoders certified by simultaneous one-sided Hoeffding bounds over every channel, transformation, attacker assignment, decoder, source, and label; delta is split equally between privacy and utility families.
methods.deterministic_mosaic: The MOSAIC confidence event and shift certificate restricted to all deterministic three-row binary-output channels.
methods.shift_unaware_mosaic: The adaptive MOSAIC confidence event with common and differential shift incorrectly set to zero; reported only as an invalid-shift ablation.
methods.mosaic: The globally optimized continuum stochastic channel with the full fine-table confidence event and registered shift model.
methods.population_oracle: The globally optimized exact external-risk channel using known population laws; an unattainable denominator.

exact_outcome.privacy: Enumerate every common-transform extreme, deterministic released-token source attacker, and source-specific residual simplex vertex; report worst normalized balanced-accuracy advantage.
exact_outcome.utility: Enumerate every common-transform extreme and residual simplex vertex; report worst source-label conditional decoder error.
exact_outcome.false_acceptance: A rule deploys and either exact privacy exceeds its scenario threshold or exact utility exceeds its scenario threshold.
exact_outcome.safe_retention: A rule deploys without exact violation in a cell where the population oracle confirms a safe opportunity.

pass_conditions.coverage: MOSAIC false acceptance is at most delta in every registered cell, with zero false acceptances on the recorded simultaneous confidence event.
pass_conditions.oracle_opportunity: The population oracle must safely deploy in every registered cell.
pass_conditions.theory_curve: Before confirmatory outcomes are read, compute both the finite-sample concentration-based abstention upper envelope and the locally stable active-set delta-method deployment curve from the locked population and code. Overlay both with the excluded-pilot calibration curve and untouched confirmation; only the concentration envelope is a finite-sample guarantee.

theory_alignment.requirements: Every finite-difference active-set diagnostic passes, each predicted curve is nondecreasing in sample size, and both registered error tolerances hold. This alignment audit is required in addition to the confirmation gate.

pass_conditions.final_synthetic_gate: Pass only if the confirmation runner's all_pass flag, the independent confirmation replay, and the preregistered theory-alignment audit all pass.

reporting.intervals: Two-sided 95% Clopper-Pearson intervals for deployment, false acceptance, and safe deployment in every cell.
reporting.replicate_receipt: Store every selected channel, decoder, exact risks, deployment decision, confidence-event marker, seed, and selection criterion.
reporting.headline: A memorable number may be quoted only from the two registered primary cells with its denominator and interval.
reporting.negative_results: All methods and cells remain in the artifact even if any pass condition fails.

stopping_rule: Run all 8000 registered independently sampled tables and all eight methods per table. No outcome-based early stopping, seed replacement, threshold replacement, scenario replacement, or sample-size replacement is permitted.
